label: true-dup
a: Using Redis sorted sets (ZSETs) for the leaderboard. ZADD updates a user score, ZRANGEBYSCORE fetches the top N, ZRANK returns a specific user position. All O(log n). No database query on the leaderboard read path.
b: Leaderboard is backed by a Redis ZSET keyed by season. Score updates call ZADD. Rank queries call ZRANGEBYSCORE. Redis sorted set operations are O(log n), which gives sub-millisecond leaderboard reads for 500k members.
reviewer_notes: Both describe Redis ZSETs as the leaderboard backing store with the same operations and O(log n) profile.
